name: Edn.Decode

aliases.name: Decoder
aliases.comment: Type alias over the function that decodes an edn value into an elm value

values.name: andMap
values.comment:
Chain decoders to apply all their results to a constructor one after the other.
Often used in conjection with "succeed" to start the chain. It is
advisable to use `map2`, `map3`, `map4`, etc for better positional error messaging.

    import Edn exposing (Edn(..))
    import Edn.Decode exposing (succeed, int, bool, decodeString)

    type alias MyRecord =
        { foo : Int
        , bar : Int
        , baz : Bool
        }

    Expect.equal
        (decodeString
            (succeed MyRecord
                |> andMap (atKey (EdnString "foo") int)
                |> andMap (atKey (EdnString "bar") int)
                |> andMap (atKey (EdnString "baz") bool)
            )
            """{"foo" 1, "bar" 2, "baz" true"}"""
        )
        (Ok ( 1, 2, True ))
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder (a -> b) -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String b

values.name: andThen
values.comment: Compose a decoder with another decoder that acts on the previous result
values.type: (a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder b) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String b

values.name: atIndex
values.type: Basics.Int -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a

values.name: atKey
values.type: Edn.Edn -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a

values.name: bool
values.comment: Decode an edn bool into an elm `Bool`
values.type: Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String Basics.Bool

values.name: char
values.comment:
Decode an edn char into an elm `Char`.

Keep in mind the way edn strings escape unicode is different than in Elm strings.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String Char.Char

values.name: decodeEdn
values.comment:
apply a decoder to a raw `Edn` value to get a result.

The `Edn` type is described in [edn-parser](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/abradley2/edn-parser/latest/Edn)
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String a

values.name: decodeString
values.comment: apply a decoder to a string value to get a result
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> String.String -> Result.Result String.String a

values.name: ednMap
values.comment:
Decode an edn map and apply two decoders to each key and value of the map
respectively. Edn maps can have keys of any type so this decodes into a list
of key/value tuples.

To decode into an Elm `Dict`, use `ednMapBy` instead.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder key -> Edn.Decode.Decoder value -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String (List.List ( key, value ))

values.name: ednMapBy
values.comment:
Decode an edn map and apply two decoders to each key and value of the map
respectively.

Accepts an additional argument that maps the keys to `comparable` types in order to return
an Elm `Dict`.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder key -> Edn.Decode.Decoder value -> (key -> comparable) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder (Dict.Dict comparable value)

values.name: fail
values.comment: Lift a failure into a decoder
values.type: String.String -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String a

values.name: float
values.comment: Decode an edn float into an elm `Float`
values.type: Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String Basics.Float

values.name: int
values.comment: Decode an edn int into an elm `Int`
values.type: Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String Basics.Int

values.name: keyword
values.comment:
Decode an edn keyword into an elm `Tuple` consisting of an optional namespace and then
the keyword
values.type: Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String ( Maybe.Maybe String.String, String.String )

values.name: list
values.comment: Decode an edn list and apply a decoder to each element of the list.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String (List.List a)

values.name: map
values.comment: Transform a value from a decoder
values.type: (a -> b) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String b

values.name: map2
values.comment:
The equivalent of chaining `andMap` twice following `succeed`

    map2 MyRecord
        (atKey (EdnString "foo") int)
        (atKey (EdnString "bar") int)

is equivalent to

    succeed MyRecord
        |> andMap (atKey (EdnString "foo") int)
        |> andMap (atKey (EdnString "bar") int)
values.type: (a -> b -> c) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder b -> Edn.Decode.Decoder c

values.name: map3
values.comment:
The equivalent of chaining `andMap` three times following `succeed`

    map3 MyRecord
        (atKey (EdnString "foo") int)
        (atKey (EdnString "bar") int)
        (atKey (EdnString "baz") bool)

is equivalent to

    succeed MyRecord
        |> andMap (atKey (EdnString "foo") int)
        |> andMap (atKey (EdnString "bar") int)
        |> andMap (atKey (EdnString "baz") bool)
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder b -> Edn.Decode.Decoder c -> Edn.Decode.Decoder d

values.name: map4
values.comment:
The equivalent of chaining `andMap` four times following `succeed`.
See the documentation of `map2` and `map3` for examples.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder b -> Edn.Decode.Decoder c -> Edn.Decode.Decoder d -> Edn.Decode.Decoder e

values.name: map5
values.comment:
The equivalent of chaining `andMap` five times following `succeed`.
See the documentation of `map2` and `map3` for examples.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder b -> Edn.Decode.Decoder c -> Edn.Decode.Decoder d -> Edn.Decode.Decoder e -> Edn.Decode.Decoder f

values.name: map6
values.comment:
The equivalent of chaining `andMap` six times following `succeed`.
See the documentation of `map2` and `map3` for examples.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f -> g) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder b -> Edn.Decode.Decoder c -> Edn.Decode.Decoder d -> Edn.Decode.Decoder e -> Edn.Decode.Decoder f -> Edn.Decode.Decoder g

values.name: map7
values.comment:
The equivalent of chaining `andMap` seven times following `succeed`.
See the documentation of `map2` and `map3` for examples.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f -> g -> h) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder b -> Edn.Decode.Decoder c -> Edn.Decode.Decoder d -> Edn.Decode.Decoder e -> Edn.Decode.Decoder f -> Edn.Decode.Decoder g -> Edn.Decode.Decoder h

values.name: map8
values.comment:
The equivalent of chaining `andMap` eight times following `succeed`.
See the documentation of `map2` and `map3` for examples.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f -> g -> h -> i) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder b -> Edn.Decode.Decoder c -> Edn.Decode.Decoder d -> Edn.Decode.Decoder e -> Edn.Decode.Decoder f -> Edn.Decode.Decoder g -> Edn.Decode.Decoder h -> Edn.Decode.Decoder i

values.name: nil
values.comment:
Decode an edn nil into `()`. This isn't super useful except for handling optional values
in combination with `oneOf`, but you likely just want to use the `optional` combinator instead.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String ()

values.name: oneOf
values.comment:
Try a list of decoders and take the first successful value or
return the first error
values.type: List.List (Edn.Decode.Decoder a) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder a

values.name: optional
values.comment:
Decode a value that may also be an edn nil into an elm `Maybe`.
This is not for decoders that may fail in any other. Use `try` for that.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Decoder (Maybe.Maybe a)

values.name: raw
values.comment:
A decoder that doesn't do anything, and just returns a raw `Edn` value.

The `Edn` type is described in [edn-parser](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/abradley2/edn-parser/latest/Edn)
values.type: Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String Edn.Edn

values.name: set
values.comment:
Decode an edn set and apply a decoder to each element of the set.

This decoder will return a list of the decoded values, not a set. To get
an Elm set, use `setBy` instead, which requires you to convert the set values
into `comparable` values.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String (List.List a)

values.name: setBy
values.comment:
Decode an edn set and apply a decoder to each element of the set.

The second argument is a function which takes each decoded item
and returns it as a `comparable` so that it may properly populate
an Elm `Set`
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> (a -> comparable) -> Edn.Decode.Decoder (Set.Set comparable)

values.name: string
values.comment: Decode an edn string into an elm `String`
values.type: Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String String.String

values.name: succeed
values.comment: Lift a value into a decoder
values.type: a -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String a

values.name: symbol
values.comment:
Symbols in edn can represent special names and operations
that are neither keywords or strings. They're generally only needed for complex situations
like DSL's

    type Operator
        = Sum
        | Prod
        | Div

    opDecoder : Decoder Operator
    opDecoder =
        oneOf
            [ symbol "+" |> map (\_ -> Sum)
            , symbol "*" |> map (\_ -> Prod)
            , symbol "/" |> map (\_ -> Div)
            ]
values.type: String.String -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String String.String

values.name: tag
values.comment:
Decode an edn tag into an elm `Tuple` consisting of a required namespace, a tag name,
and then a nested edn value for the decoder argument. Only really useful if you are unaware
of the tag being received. You will usually want to use `tagTransform` or `tagWith` instead.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String ( String.String, String.String, a )

values.name: tagTransform
values.comment:
Decode a specific tag with a given namespace and tagname, and apply a transform that may
fail
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> ( String.String, String.String ) -> (a -> Result.Result String.String b) -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String b

values.name: tagWith
values.comment: Decode a specific tag with a given namespace and a tagname
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> ( String.String, String.String ) -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String a

values.name: try
values.comment:
Force a decoder to succeed by nesting the `Result` returned in an `Ok` value.
Useful for ignoring errors for particularly troublesome data without losing the
error details.
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String (Result.Result String.String a)

values.name: vector
values.comment: Decode an edn vector into an Elm `Array`, applying the decoder to each item
values.type: Edn.Decode.Decoder a -> Edn.Decode.Context -> Edn.Edn -> Result.Result String.String (Array.Array a)
